mwivhet 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

mwivhet: Inference with Many Weak Instruments and Heterogeneity

Inference in a linear instrumental variable regression model with many potentially weak instruments, in the presence of heterogeneous treatment effects. The package implements procedures from Yap (2025).

The example below uses the bundled Suffolk County prosecution data. For additional documentation, please refer to the package website.

Installation

You can install the development version from GitHub with:

if (!requireNamespace("remotes")) {
  install.packages("remotes")
}
remotes::install_github("minhnngo/mwivhet")

Citation

If you use mwivhet, please cite: https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2408.11193

Suffolk County Application

The following code shows the application of the method in investigating the effect of misdemeanor prosecution (X) on criminal complaint in two years (Y), based on the paper Misdemeanor Prosecution (Agan et al., 2023). 

library(mwivhet)
library(dplyr)
library(fixest)

# 0. Setup ----------------------------------------------------------------
df <- suffolk

# 1. Preparation ----------------------------------------------------------
# 1.1. Variable Assignment
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    X = ng_immed_all,
    Y = anyr_twoyears_arrest2,
    groupZ = first_pros
  )

# 1.2. Construct Covariate Groups (groupW)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    monthID = as.integer(factor(court_month2)),
    dowID   = as.integer(factor(court_dow2)),
    groupW  = as.integer(factor(paste(dowID, monthID, sep = "_")))
  )

# 1.3. Construct Interaction Groups (groupQ / group)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    groupQ = as.integer(factor(paste(groupW, groupZ, sep = "_"))),
    group  = groupQ
  )

# 1.4. Filter for Group Size
df <- df %>%
  group_by(groupQ) %>%
  filter(n() >= 4) %>%
  ungroup()


# 1.5. Get residualized objects
lmX <- fixest::feols(X~1|group,data=df)
df$MX <- lmX$residuals

lmY <- fixest::feols(Y~1|group,data=df)
df$MY <- lmY$residuals

# 2. Inference ------------------------------------------------------------
S_hat <- GetLM(df, X, X, groupW, group, noisy = FALSE)
JIVE <- GetLM(df, X, Y, groupW, group, noisy = FALSE)/S_hat 

L3OCIcoef <- GetCIcoef(df, groupW, group, X, Y, MX, MY, noisy = FALSE)
L3OCI <- GetCItypebd(L3OCIcoef)[2:3] 

# 3. Table ----------------------------------------------------------------
# 3.1. Create the matrix using calculated L3O variables
L3O_data <- matrix(c(
  L3OCI[1],                # Lower Bound
  L3OCI[2],                # Upper Bound
  JIVE,                    # Point Estimate
  L3OCI[2] - L3OCI[1]      # CI Length
), ncol = 1)

# 3.2. Add labels for row/col
rownames(L3O_data) <- c("LB", "UB", "Estimate", "CIlength")
colnames(L3O_data) <- "L3O"

# 3.3 Round and save
CItab_L3O_round <- round(L3O_data, 3)
CItab_L3O_round
#>             L3O
#> LB       -0.222
#> UB       -0.066
#> Estimate -0.144
#> CIlength  0.155
